Motion For Protective Order
Off Calendar Aug-24-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, David L. Hart, Esq. (650-355-3000) appears as counsel for plaintiff/opposing party

Attorney, Helen H. Chen of Robinson & Wood, Inc. (408-298-7120) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

The cause is argued and submitted by both sides as set forth in the transcript of the certified shorthand reporter. The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

Defendants, Karlyn Kenneth Storms and Costco Wholesale Corporation's Motion for a Protective Order to Reduce the Number of Plaintiff's Non-Retained Experts is GRANTED in part. No later than August 26, 2016 plaintiff must state in writing which of the non-retained experts he intends to call in his case in chief and to state how the testimony of each such person is not cumulative of the testimony of all other persons that the plaintiff intends to call in his case in chief. Failure to include any person in this writing precludes plaintiff from calling that person as a non-retained expert witness at trial. Defendants may, at their option, depose any or all persons identified in the writing and/or file a motion in limine with the trial judge seeking to exclude the testimony of any or all such persons. Prevailing party to submit a [proposed] written order in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR #6576, bus. tel: (530) 339-5023, email: rhunter@sftc.org. (D302)
